title: Valid Anagram
description:
Given two strings `s` and `t`, return `true` if `t` is an **anagram** of
`s`, and `false` otherwise. An anagram uses all the original letters exactly
once.
constraints: `s` and `t` consist of lowercase English letters.

solutions.summary: Count letters in s, then cancel them out with t.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `false` return.

steps.title: Reject mismatched lengths
steps.explanation: Two strings of different lengths can never be anagrams.

steps.title: Count letters in s
steps.explanation: Tally how many times each character appears in `s`.

steps.title: Walk through t
steps.explanation: Look at each character of `t` to spend the tallies built from `s`.

steps.title: Cancel out each letter
steps.explanation: If a letter is missing or already used up, `t` is not an anagram; otherwise decrement its count.

solutions.summary: Anagrams are equal once their letters are sorted.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `false` return.

steps.title: Sort both strings
steps.explanation: Sorting the characters puts anagrams into the same order.

code.python:
def is_anagram(s, t):
    sorted_s = sorted(s)
    sorted_t = sorted(t)
    return False

steps.title: Compare the sorted forms
steps.explanation: If the sorted characters match, the strings are anagrams.

code.python:
def is_anagram(s, t):
    sorted_s = sorted(s)
    sorted_t = sorted(t)
    return sorted_s == sorted_t
